case_title: DAVID VETTER et al VS. DARREN BECHTEL et al

docket_entries.description: 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF VETTER, DAVID VETTER, GENE RIBEIRO, EXPEDITO COYNE, MARY VETTER CURROTTO, MICHAEL LEE, ROBERTA THE SNC TRUST AS TO DEFENDANT BECHTEL, DARREN DOES 1 TO 100
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED SUSTAINING DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAIANT FILED BY DEFENDANT BECHTEL, DARREN

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DARREN BECHTEL'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. AS TO THE FIRST (FRAUD) AND FOURTH (PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL) CAUSES OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ("SAC") ALLEGES WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY THAT PLAINTIFFS JUSTIFIABLY RELIED TO THEIR DETRIMENT ON A FALSE PROMISE BY DEFENDANT AND AS A RESULT OF THAT RELIANCE SUFFERED DAMAGES. THE SAC PLEADS THAT DEFENDANT FALSELY PROMISED TO PURCHASE PLAINTIFFS' SHARES. (SAC 105, 110, 121.) RELYING ON THIS, PLAINTIFFS GAVE DEFENDANT PROXIES TO VOTE THEIR SHARES, WHICH ALLOWED DEFENDANT TO CONTROL THE COMPANY. (SAC 113, 118, 122.) ONCE IN CONTROL, PS SHARES WERE "REDUCED TO BOOK VALUE UNILATERALLY ASSIGNED BY DEFENDANT BETCHEL TO NO MORE THAN $ 187,596 INSTEAD OF PAYING THE AGREED SUM OF $677,945." ( 135.) AS TO THE SECOND (NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION) CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE SAC ALLEGES A FALSE PROMISE, NOT A MISREPRESENTATION OF A PAST OR EXISTING MATERIAL FACT. A FALSE PROMISE CANNOT BE THE BASIS OF AN ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION. (TARMANN V. STATE FARM MUT. AUTO. INS. CO. (1991) 2 CAL.APP.4TH 153, 159.) (COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: RICHARD VAUGHT, ESQ., 408.275.8523, FOR PLAINTIFFS.
RACHAEL TURNER, ESQ., 415.954.4400, FOR DEFENDANT.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT DARREN BECHTEL's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the first (Fraud) and fourth (Promissory Estoppel) causes of action, the demurrer is overruled. The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") alleges with sufficient specificity that Plaintiffs justifiably relied to their detriment on a false promise by Defendant and as a result of that reliance suffered damages. The SAC pleads that Defendant falsely promised to purchase Plaintiffs' shares. (SAC 105, 110, 121.) Relying on this, Plaintiffs gave Defendant proxies to vote their shares, which allowed Defendant to control the company. (SAC 113, 118, 122.) Once in control, Ps shares were "reduced to book value unilaterally assigned by defendant Betchel to no more than $ 187,596 instead of paying the agreed sum of $677,945." ( 135.) As to the second (Negligent Misrepresentation) cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The SAC alleges a false promise, not a misrepresentation of a past or existing material fact. A false promise cannot be the basis of an action for negligent misrepresentation. (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 159.) (Counsel for plaintiff to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: RICHARD VAUGHT, ESQ., 408.275.8523, FOR PLAINTIFFS.
RACHAEL TURNER, ESQ., 415.954.4400, FOR DEFENDANT.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT DARREN BECHTEL's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted. The Second Amended Complaint does not allege a statutory or contractual basis for the recovery of attorney's fees. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
